stem: [[n]] distinct [[noun]] are to be arranged in a single row. In how many of these arrangements are two particular individuals seated immediately next to each other?
solution: Glue the two individuals into one block, giving \( ([[n]]-1)! \) arrangements of the [[n]]-1 objects, then \( 2! \) internal orders: \( ([[n]]-1)!\cdot 2! = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the total number of arrangements with no restriction.
This treats the glued block but forgets the \( 2! \) internal orders of the pair.
This counts the arrangements in which the two are NOT adjacent (the complement).
This shrinks the block count to \( ([[n]]-2)! \), removing one too many positions.
This doubles the total instead of the block's internal arrangements.
This is off from the correct count by [[n]].

stem: [[n]] distinct [[noun]] are to be arranged in a single row. In how many of these arrangements are two particular individuals NOT seated next to each other?
solution: Of the \( [[n]]! \) total arrangements, \( ([[n]]-1)!\cdot 2! \) have the pair adjacent (glue-block count); subtracting gives \( [[n]]! - ([[n]]-1)!\cdot 2! = [[answer]] \) arrangements with the pair apart.
distractor_rationales: This is the count of arrangements where the pair IS adjacent, the complement of what was asked.
This is the total number of arrangements with no restriction at all.
This treats only one individual as fixed, not the pair's adjacency count.
This subtracts the wrong glue-block count (missing the internal \( 2! \) order).
This is off from the correct count by [[n]].
This is off from the correct count by [[n]].

stem: [[n]] distinct [[noun]] are arranged in a single row. Among them, a particular group of [[m]] individuals is singled out. In how many of these arrangements is no pair of the [[m]] singled-out individuals seated next to each other?
solution: First seat the other \( [[n]]-[[m]] \) individuals: \( ([[n]]-[[m]])! \) ways. This creates \( [[n]]-[[m]]+1 \) gaps (including the ends); placing the [[m]] singled-out individuals into distinct gaps, in order, uses \( \frac{([[n]]-[[m]]+1)!}{([[n]]-2[[m]]+1)!} \) of them. Multiplying gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the total number of arrangements with no restriction.
This treats the singled-out group as one glued block instead of spreading them into separate gaps.
This seats only the other individuals and forgets to place the singled-out group at all.
This multiplies the two factorials without dividing out the unused gap choices.
This is off from the correct count by [[m]].
This is off from the correct count by [[n]].

stem: [[n]] distinct [[noun]] are arranged in a single row. Two disjoint designated pairs are marked: pair A and pair B (four different individuals in total). In how many arrangements are the two members of pair A seated next to each other AND the two members of pair B seated next to each other?
solution: Glue pair A into one block and pair B into another, leaving \( [[n]]-2 \) objects to arrange in \( ([[n]]-2)! \) ways, times \( 2! \) internal orders for each of the two blocks: \( ([[n]]-2)!\cdot 2!\cdot 2! = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This glues only one of the two pairs and leaves the other unrestricted.
This is the total number of arrangements with no restriction.
This shrinks the object count to \( [[n]]-3 \), removing one too many positions.
This forgets the internal \( 2! \) order of the second glued pair.
This is off from the correct count by [[n]].
This is off from the correct count by [[n]].

stem: In a row of [[n]] seats, [[m]] [[group_a]] and [[m]] [[group_b]] (all distinct individuals) are to be seated so that no two people from the same group are seated next to each other. In how many ways can this be done?
solution: Either the boys or the girls can occupy the odd-numbered seats (2 choices of pattern), and within each pattern the [[m]] boys can be arranged in \( [[m]]! \) ways and the [[m]] girls in \( [[m]]! \) ways: \( 2\cdot [[m]]!\cdot [[m]]! = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor of 2 for which gender starts the row.
This is the total number of unrestricted arrangements of all [[n]] people.
This uses an unrelated formula mixing a doubled factorial of one fewer seat.
This double-counts the starting-gender choice by using 4 instead of 2.
This is off from the correct count by [[m]].
This is off from the correct count by [[m]].
